> *Your current cash is just $1,900 short of a full six-month reserve.*

You should target **$32,400** in emergency savings. Your average spending from **July 2025 through June 2026** was **$5,400 per month**, making the usual range:

| Reserve level | Amount |
|---|---:|
| 3-month floor | $16,200 |
| **6-month target** | **$32,400** |
| Current checking and savings | $30,500 |
| **Remaining gap** | **$1,900** |

Your current cash covers about **5.6 months**. Given that your household includes two adults and two young children, I recommend completing the six-month reserve rather than stopping at three months. At your average **$900 monthly surplus**, you can close the gap in a little over **two months**.

Keep the reserve in an **FDIC-insured high-yield savings account**, separate from everyday checking. If any of the $30,500 is already reserved for upcoming bills or another purpose, subtract it when measuring your emergency fund.

Only one financial institution is visible, so your true income, spending, or cash reserves may be higher. Connecting any other bank accounts would confirm the target against your complete cash flow: [Connect an account](/domains/new).
